framework.name: Dify
framework.description: Visual DAG-based low-code AI application platform. Drag-and-drop canvas for building LLM workflows (Chatflow + Workflow modes). Agent node supports ReAct and Function Calling strategies. Enterprise features: knowledge base RAG, tool plugin ecosystem, conversation logs, annotation-based fine-tuning.

modules.name: Visual Canvas
modules.description: Drag-and-drop workflow editor. Users compose Chatflow (conversational) or Workflow (batch pipeline) by connecting nodes. Auto-saves to backend.

modules.name: DAG Engine
modules.description: Workflow runtime orchestrator. Validates DAG topology, manages execution order, handles branching (IF/ELSE) and iteration (Iteration node), resolves variable references.

modules.name: Node Executor
modules.description: Per-node execution sandbox. Each node type has a registered executor: LLM, Knowledge Retrieval, Tool, Code (Python/JS sandbox), HTTP Request, Template transform.

modules.name: Context Pool
modules.description: Shared state container across the workflow run. Stores node outputs, conversation variables, session metadata. All nodes read/write via variable references.

modules.name: LLM Node
modules.description: Model invocation node: system + user prompt with variable injection, memory window settings, vision support. Wraps provider SDKs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.).

modules.name: Knowledge Retrieval
modules.description: RAG pipeline node: embedding → vector search → rerank. Connects to Knowledge Bases (uploaded documents via chunking + indexing pipeline).

modules.name: Tool Node
modules.description: Plugin ecosystem node: 100+ built-in tools (search, image gen, code exec, APIs). Custom tools via OpenAPI spec or Python plugin SDK.

modules.name: Agent Node
modules.description: Autonomous agent loop: ReAct (reasoning + acting) or Function Calling strategy. Has access to tools, knowledge, and LLM. Iterates until Final Answer.

modules.name: IF/ELSE Node
modules.description: Conditional branching on variable values. Supports multiple conditions (AND/OR logic) with true/false output branches. Enables dynamic workflow paths.

modules.name: Code Node
modules.description: Custom code execution in sandboxed Python3 or JavaScript. Receives inputs as args, returns structured output. Used for data transformation and custom logic.

modules.name: Iteration Node
modules.description: Loop container: iterates over array variable, executes child nodes for each item. Output aggregated into array. Enables batch processing patterns.

modules.name: HTTP Request Node
modules.description: External API call node: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE with headers, body, authorization. Response parsed into variables for downstream nodes.

modules.name: Variable Aggregator
modules.description: Merges multiple branch outputs into single variable. Used after IF/ELSE to converge divergent paths back into one flow.

metadata.description: User publishes canvas; DAG serialized to backend, validated for topology
DAG Engine resolves next runnable node(s) based on dependencies and dispatches to executor
Executor invokes LLM node; model response returned with token usage
Executor queries Knowledge node; embedding search + rerank returns relevant chunks
Executor invokes Tool node; external API/tool response returned
Executor runs code in sandboxed Python/JS environment; returns structured output
Executor fires HTTP request to external service; response parsed into variables
Executor launches agent; ReAct/FunctionCalling loop runs until Final Answer
Agent node calls LLM for reasoning step; receives thought + action decision
Agent invokes tool during ReAct cycle; tool result feeds next reasoning step
Agent queries knowledge base for context during reasoning loop
Node output written to Context Pool; downstream nodes read via variable references
DAG Engine evaluates IF/ELSE conditions against Context Pool variables, selects branch
DAG Engine iterates over array, executes child nodes per item, aggregates results
Both IF and ELSE branch outputs converge at Variable Aggregator before continuing
DAG Engine reads context to determine next nodes and resolve variable references
Executor signals node completion; DAG Engine determines next node(s) in topology

phases.name: Canvas Design & Deploy
phases.description: User designs workflow on visual canvas; Dify serializes, validates, and registers the DAG

steps.description: User arranges nodes and connects them on the Visual Canvas
Canvas serializes workflow graph to JSON and sends to backend
DAG Engine validates: no cycles, all variable refs resolvable, all required inputs connected

phases.name: Workflow Execution
phases.description: DAG Engine orchestrates node execution depth-first respecting dependencies; branches and loops handled

steps.description: DAG Engine resolves entry nodes and initializes Context Pool with input variables
DAG Engine reads Context Pool to resolve variable references for current node(s)
DAG Engine dispatches next ready node(s) to Node Executor for execution
Node Executor runs the node (LLM/KB/Tool/Code/HTTP/Agent) in appropriate sandbox
Node output stored in Context Pool for downstream consumption
After IF/ELSE: evaluate conditions against context, select branch. After Iteration: loop or proceed.
DAG Engine checks for remaining unvisited nodes; continues loop or marks workflow complete
